title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for search metadata, indexing directives, canonical signals, and language alternates and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for search discovery and indexability audit record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.
Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Indexability Decision
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz site-quality team handles case 8006 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: A technically crawlable page is guaranteed to be indexed. That claim fails the indexability decision · changed case evidence boundary for Indexability Decision.

For indexability decision · changed case, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Indexability Decision for search discovery and indexability audit record.

Report status, content type, robots directives, canonical signals, authentication, rendered state, and search-engine-specific limits as evidence, not a guaranteed index outcome. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for search discovery and indexability audit record.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Title Description Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Title Description Evidence Authorized crawling and Python authorization-to-recovery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

content.text: A quiz site-quality team handles case 9816 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile search metadata, robots directives, canonical signals, language alternates, and indexability non-claims; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Indexability Decision

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation.
